title: Copilot CLI Cheat Sheet
description: A practical guide for engineers using Copilot CLI for efficient coding assistance.

commands.scenario: When starting a new project and you want to set up Copilot quickly.

commands.description: Deploy a service to your environment.
commands.scenario: When you need to deploy changes to a service in your application.

commands.description: Create a new environment for your application.
commands.scenario: When setting up a new environment for testing or production.

commands.description: Set up a new pipeline for continuous delivery.
commands.scenario: When automating the deployment process for your application.

commands.description: Update an existing pipeline with new configurations.
commands.scenario: When changes are made to the pipeline configuration and need to be applied.

commands.description: Show details about a deployed service.
commands.scenario: When you need to inspect the status or configuration of a service.

commands.description: Display information about the application and its services.
commands.scenario: When reviewing the overall setup of your application and its components.

commands.description: Add storage resources to your service.
commands.scenario: When your service needs to interact with databases or S3 buckets.
When you need to securely store and access sensitive information.

commands.description: Remove a service from your application.
commands.scenario: When a service is no longer needed and should be removed.

commands.description: Delete an environment and all its resources.
commands.scenario: When an environment is no longer needed and should be cleaned up.

commands.description: Delete an application and all its resources.
commands.scenario: When you need to completely remove an application and its infrastructure.

commands.description: Stream real-time service logs with a tail buffer to debug runtime exceptions and deployment failures immediately.
Export the entire environment configuration into a machine-readable JSON format for version control or disaster recovery.
Perform a syntax and logic check on pipeline definitions without executing the actual deployment steps.
Dynamically adjust application resource allocation and scaling strategies to optimize cost and availability.
Trigger an immediate rotation of sensitive credentials to mitigate security risks during potential leaks.
